titleStmt.title: Letter from Henry Arthur Wynne to Samuel Alex Wray, 23 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Henry Arthur Wynne

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Henry Arthur Wynne to Samuel Alex Wray (b.1875), a solicitor. The letter is a reply of a previous letter sent by Wray regarding Professor Valentine Steinberger (b.1853), a German professor in University College Galway who was arrested following the Easter Rising and deported to Wandsworth prison, for whom Wray is solicitor. The previous letter requested that Wray be allowed consult Steinberger regarding rent payments and also ask about his whereabouts. In reply, Wynne simply states that Steinberger is in the custody of military authorities.Henry Arthur Wynne (1867-1945) was the chief crown solicitor for Ireland following the death of Malchy Kelly (1950-1916).

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5611/8624/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
8624 23rd May 1916 Dear Sir,
In reply to your letter of 19th instant I have to
inform you that as Professor Steinberger is in the custody of
the Military Authorities it will be necessary for you to
communicate with them in the matter. Yours truly, Henry Arthur Wynne
Samuel A Wray Esq.,
Solicitor,
Coleraine.
